title: Rule-based evolution engine with integrity cap
acceptance_criteria: Exports a TypeScript interface: EvolutionResult = { action: 'none' | 'proposed' | 'applied' | 'skipped'; proposedValue?: number; reason?: string }
Exports `runEvolutionEngine(pool: Pool, personalityId: string, tradeDateISO: string, metrics: { winRate: number; totalTrades: number; totalPnlPct: number }): Promise<EvolutionResult>`
EVOLUTION_REQUIRE_APPROVAL fail-safe: const requireApproval = process.env.EVOLUTION_REQUIRE_APPROVAL !== 'false' — approval is required unless the env var is explicitly set to the string 'false'
Rule — lower threshold fires when: metrics.winRate < 0.4 AND metrics.totalTrades >= 20 — delta = -0.05
Rule — raise threshold fires when: metrics.winRate > 0.7 AND metrics.totalTrades >= 20 — delta = +0.03
If no rule fires OR metrics.totalTrades < 20, returns { action: 'none' } immediately without any DB writes

All write operations are inside a single withTransaction(pool, async (client) => { ... }) call — the transaction spans the SELECT FOR UPDATE, the integrity cap computation, the cooldown check, and the final write

Inside the transaction, locks ALL active momentum_exhaustion personalities simultaneously: SELECT id, name, is_frozen, entry_type, is_active, params, last_evolved_at FROM personality_configs WHERE entry_type = 'momentum_exhaustion' AND is_active = TRUE FOR UPDATE — this prevents TOCTOU race conditions on the spread computation

Identifies the target personality from the locked rows by matching id = personalityId — if the target is not found in the locked set, throws Error('personality ' + personalityId + ' not found in momentum_exhaustion group')
If target's is_frozen = TRUE, throws new Error('FROZEN_VIOLATION: cannot evolve frozen personality ' + name)

Reads current min_probability: const minProb = Number((params as any).min_probability) — guards: if (!Number.isFinite(minProb)) { log warning; return { action: 'skipped', reason: 'min_probability_not_finite' } }
Computes proposedValue = Math.max(0.30, Math.min(0.90, minProb + delta)) — clamps to [0.30, 0.90] hard floor/ceiling

Comparison integrity cap (KEY USER DECISION — never block, only cap): simulates the locked rows array with the target's min_probability replaced by proposedValue; computes the spread = max(all min_probability values) - min(all min_probability values); if spread > 0.08, adjusts proposedValue to maintain exactly 0.08 spread (for a decrease: proposedValue = maxOtherProb - 0.08; for an increase: proposedValue = minOtherProb + 0.08); then re-clamps proposedValue to [0.30, 0.90]

After the integrity cap: if proposedValue === minProb (rounded to 6 decimal places to avoid floating-point noise), returns { action: 'none', reason: 'integrity_cap_no_change' } — the cap absorbed the entire adjustment

7-day cooldown check (inside the transaction, using the already-locked row's last_evolved_at): parses last_evolved_at to IST date string using toLocaleDateString('en-CA', { timeZone: 'Asia/Kolkata' }); computes day difference; if last_evolved_at IS NOT NULL and diff < 7, returns { action: 'skipped', reason: 'cooldown' }

If requireApproval = true: UPDATE retrospection_results SET proposed_adjustments = $1::jsonb, proposed_adjustments_at = NOW(), adjustments_applied = FALSE WHERE personality_id = $2 AND trade_date = $3 — the proposed_adjustments JSON is { min_probability: proposedValue, rule: 'lower_threshold' | 'raise_threshold', original: minProb }; returns { action: 'proposed', proposedValue }

If requireApproval = false: UPDATE personality_configs SET params = jsonb_set(params, '{min_probability}', to_json($1::float8)::jsonb), last_evolved_at = NOW(), evolution_consecutive_applications = evolution_consecutive_applications + 1 WHERE id = $2; INSERT INTO personality_audit_log (id, personality_id, changed_at, changed_by, old_params, new_params, reason) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), $1, NOW(), 'evolution-engine', $2::jsonb, $3::jsonb, $4); returns { action: 'applied', proposedValue }
Imports withTransaction from '../db/client.js' and Pool from 'pg'
No default exports — named exports only
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
